steps.title: Decode 'exaggerated sense of abilities'
steps.content: This phrase means Proudie thinks he's more capable/talented than he is—his self-perception is inflated. The evidence quote must reveal his delusional belief in his own gifts, not describe his actual abilities.

steps.title: Spot the self-deception
steps.content: Choice B states “fondly flattered himself, been selected as possessing such gifts, natural and acquired, as must be sure to recommend him to a yet higher notice.” The phrase “fondly flattered himself” directly shows Proudie convincing himself about his talents. The other choices either state his actual inadequacy (A), list objective circumstances (C), or describe merely adequate ability (D). That matches choice B — B is correct.
